text.title: Nomad Low-Energy Mode Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Nomad Low-Energy Mode. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Low-energy mode caps founder daily work ≤2 hrs. More = not low-energy mode = trying to do both.
rule.rationale: Two hours is the band where decisions remain coherent and recovery is preserved.

rule.statement: Pre-declare 3 allowed task types in low-energy mode (e.g. async-standup-read, contractor-block-escalation, customer-emergency). Everything else queues.
rule.rationale: Without explicit allowed list, low-energy collapses into 'whatever feels urgent'.

rule.statement: Email + Slack auto-reply states mode + return date + escalation contact. Silence is worse than the truth.
rule.rationale: Auto-reply manages expectations; silence breeds churn + escalation.

rule.statement: Named escalation contact for hard cases. 'Reach out if X' — explicit threshold.
rule.rationale: Without explicit contact, low-energy founder gets pinged anyway.

rule.statement: Mode deactivated by explicit switch + return-debrief (what was paused, what to catch up on). Implicit deactivation = stuff stays stale.
rule.rationale: Implicit return = quiet backlog rot; explicit deactivation forces debrief.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-nomad-low-energy-mode.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
